front: Novel: begins with a parrot squawking 'Allez vous-en!' and ends with the protagonist drowning herself in the Gulf of Mexico
Novel: the protagonist has an affair with Robert Lebrun, who leaves a note 'Good-by - because I love you' in the 'pigeon house' she moves into
Novel: the protagonist breaks a glass vase, stomps on her wedding ring, keeps a picture of a 'great tragedian,' and befriends the pianist Mademoiselle Reisz
Short Story: Armand Aubigny expels his wife and baby when the child resembles a 'quadroon' boy; a letter reveals that Armand himself has Black ancestry
Short Story: the title woman disappears into the bayou after her husband burns a willow cradle at the L'Abri estate
Short Story: Louise Mallard whispers 'free, free, free!' after hearing her husband died in a railroad accident, then dies of heart disease when he returns alive
back: The Story of an Hour (Kate Chopin)

front: Short Story: doctors say she died 'of heart disease -- of the joy that kills'
back: The Story of an Hour (Kate Chopin)

front: Short Story: Mrs. Sommers spends fifteen dollars on kid gloves, stockings, and a matinee instead of buying clothes for her children
back: A Pair of Silk Stockings (Kate Chopin)

front: Short Story: a sexual encounter between Alcee and Calixta during a rainstorm; sequel to 'At the 'Cadian Ball'
Novel: the sawmill manager David pursues the Creole widow Therese Lafirme; Fanny drowns while scavenging for alcohol
